sn54.16:1.1.pli: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te bhikkhū bhagavā etadavoca:
sn54.16:1.1.eng: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to them:

sn54.16:1.2.pli: “atthi nu kho, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro dhamme paripūreti, cattāro dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti, satta dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā dve dhamme paripūrentī”ti?
sn54.16:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, is there one thing that, when developed and cultivated, fulfills four things; and those four things, when developed and cultivated, fulfill seven things; and those seven things, when developed and cultivated, fulfill two things?”

sn54.16:1.3.pli: “Bhagavaṁmūlakā no, bhante, dhammā …pe… bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
sn54.16:1.3.eng: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”

sn54.16:1.4.pli: “Atthi, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro dhamme paripūreti, cattāro dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti, satta dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā dve dhamme paripūrenti.

sn54.16:2.1.pli: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro dhamme paripūreti, cattāro dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta dhamme paripūrenti, satta dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā dve dhamme paripūrenti?
sn54.16:2.1.eng: And what is that one thing?

sn54.16:2.2.pli: Ānāpānassatisamādhi, bhikkhave, ekadhammo bhāvito bahulīkato cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūreti, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvitā bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti, satta bojjhaṅgā bhāvitā bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrentīti.
sn54.16:2.2.eng: Immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is one thing that, when developed and cultivated, fulfills the four kinds of mindfulness meditation. And the four kinds of mindfulness meditation, when developed and cultivated, fulfill the seven awakening factors. And the seven awakening factors, when developed and cultivated, fulfill knowledge and freedom.

sn54.16:3.1.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvito ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhi kathaṁ bahulīkato cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūreti?
sn54.16:3.1.eng: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated so as to fulfill the four kinds of mindfulness meditation?

sn54.16:3.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
sn54.16:3.2.eng: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence. …

sn54.16:4.1.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dīghaṁ vā assasanto ‘dīghaṁ assasāmī’ti pajānāti, dīghaṁ vā passasanto ‘dīghaṁ passasāmī’ti pajānāti, rassaṁ vā assasanto ‘rassaṁ assasāmī’ti pajānāti …pe…

sn54.16:4.3.pli: ‘passambhayaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘passambhayaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—

sn54.16:4.4.pli: kāye kāyānupassī, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:4.7.pli: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, kāye kāyānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:5.4.pli: ‘passambhayaṁ cittasaṅkhāraṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘passambhayaṁ cittasaṅkhāraṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—

sn54.16:5.5.pli: vedanāsu vedanānupassī, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:5.7.pli: Vedanāññatarāhaṁ, bhikkhave, etaṁ vadāmi, yadidaṁ—assāsapassāsānaṁ sādhukaṁ manasikāraṁ.

sn54.16:5.8.pli: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, vedanāsu vedanānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:6.3.pli: ‘samādahaṁ cittaṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘samādahaṁ cittaṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati;

sn54.16:6.4.pli: ‘vimocayaṁ cittaṁ assasissāmī’ti sikkhati, ‘vimocayaṁ cittaṁ passasissāmī’ti sikkhati—

sn54.16:6.5.pli: citte cittānupassī, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:6.7.pli: Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, muṭṭhassatissa asampajānassa ānāpānassatisamādhibhāvanaṁ vadāmi.

sn54.16:6.8.pli: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, citte cittānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:7.5.pli: dhammesu dhammānupassī, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:7.6.pli: So yaṁ taṁ hoti abhijjhādomanassānaṁ pahānaṁ taṁ paññāya disvā sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.

sn54.16:7.7.pli: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, dhammesu dhammānupassī bhikkhu tasmiṁ samaye viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.

sn54.16:8.1.pli: Evaṁ bhāvito kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhi evaṁ bahulīkato cattāro satipaṭṭhāne paripūreti.

sn54.16:9.1.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā kathaṁ bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti?

sn54.16:9.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati—upaṭṭhitāssa tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno sati hoti asammuṭṭhā.

sn54.16:9.3.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno upaṭṭhitā sati asammuṭṭhā—

sn54.16:9.4.pli: satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, satisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:10.1.pli: So tathāsato viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati.

sn54.16:10.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathāsato viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati—

sn54.16:10.3.pli: dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:11.1.pli: Tassa taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinato pavicarato parivīmaṁsamāpajjato āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ.

sn54.16:11.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinato pavicarato parivīmaṁsamāpajjato āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ—

sn54.16:11.3.pli: vīriyasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, vīriyasambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, vīriyasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:12.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno āraddhavīriyassa uppajjati pīti nirāmisā—

sn54.16:12.3.pli: pītisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, pītisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, pītisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:13.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pītimanassa kāyopi passambhati, cittampi passambhati—

sn54.16:13.3.pli: passaddhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, passaddhisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, passaddhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:14.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno passaddhakāyassa sukhino cittaṁ samādhiyati—

sn54.16:14.3.pli: samādhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, samādhisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, samādhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:15.1.pli: So tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.

sn54.16:15.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti—

sn54.16:15.3.pli: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:16.3.pli: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati—upaṭṭhitāssa tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno sati hoti asammuṭṭhā.

sn54.16:16.4.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno upaṭṭhitā sati hoti asammuṭṭhā—

sn54.16:16.5.pli: satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, satisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti—satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati …pe….

sn54.16:17.1.pli: So tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.

sn54.16:17.2.pli: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti—

sn54.16:17.3.pli: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.

sn54.16:17.4.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, cattāro satipaṭṭhānā evaṁ bahulīkatā satta bojjhaṅge paripūrenti.

sn54.16:18.1.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, satta bojjhaṅgā kathaṁ bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrenti?

sn54.16:18.2.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ;

sn54.16:18.3.pli: dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…

sn54.16:18.4.pli: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn54.16:18.5.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, satta bojjhaṅgā evaṁ bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiṁ paripūrentī”ti.
sn54.16:18.5.eng: That’s how the seven awakening factors are developed and cultivated so as to fulfill knowledge and freedom.”

sn54.17:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it leads to giving up the fetters …”

sn54.18:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it leads to uprooting the underlying tendencies …”

sn54.19:0.3.eng: Completely Understanding the Course of Time

sn54.19:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it leads to completely understanding the course of time …”

sn54.2:1.1.pli: “Ānāpānassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
sn54.2:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.

sn54.2:1.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?
sn54.2:1.2.eng: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated to be very fruitful and beneficial?

sn54.2:1.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ānāpānassatisahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ,
sn54.2:1.3.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops mindfulness of breathing together with the awakening factors of mindfulness,

sn54.2:1.4.eng: investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,

sn54.2:1.5.pli: ānāpānassatisahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn54.2:1.5.eng: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn54.2:1.6.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā”ti.
sn54.2:1.6.eng: Mindfulness of breathing, when developed and cultivated in this way, is very fruitful and beneficial.”

sn54.20:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it leads to the ending of defilements.

sn54.20:1.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvito ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhi kathaṁ bahulīkato saṁyojanappahānāya saṁvattati …
sn54.20:1.2.eng: And how is immersion due to mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated so as to lead to giving up the fetters,

sn54.20:1.4.eng: completely understanding the course of time,

sn54.20:1.6.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā …pe…
sn54.20:1.6.eng: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in front of them. …

sn54.20:1.7.pli: paṭinissaggānupassī assasissāmīti sikkhati, paṭinissaggānupassī passasissāmīti sikkhati.
sn54.20:1.7.eng: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’

sn54.20:1.8.pli: Evaṁ bhāvito kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhi evaṁ bahulīkato saṁyojanappahānāya saṁvattati …pe…
sn54.20:1.8.eng: That’s how immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated so as to lead to giving up the fetters,

sn54.20:1.10.eng: completely understanding the course of time,

sn54.20:2.5.eng: The Linked Discourses on Mindfulness of Breathing is the tenth section.

sn54.3:1.1.pli: “Ānāpānassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
sn54.3:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.

sn54.3:1.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?
sn54.3:1.2.eng: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated to be very fruitful and beneficial?

sn54.3:1.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
sn54.3:1.3.eng: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.

sn54.3:1.4.eng: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. …

sn54.3:1.5.eng: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’

sn54.3:1.6.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā”ti.
sn54.3:1.6.eng: Mindfulness of breathing, when developed and cultivated in this way, is very fruitful and beneficial.”

sn54.4:1.1.pli: “Ānāpānassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
sn54.4:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.

sn54.4:1.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?
sn54.4:1.2.eng: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated to be very fruitful and beneficial?

sn54.4:1.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
sn54.4:1.3.eng: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.

sn54.4:1.4.eng: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. …

sn54.4:1.5.eng: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’

sn54.4:1.6.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
sn54.4:1.6.eng: Mindfulness of breathing, when developed and cultivated in this way, is very fruitful and beneficial.

sn54.4:1.7.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitāya kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
sn54.4:1.7.eng: When mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated in this way you can expect one of two results:

sn54.4:1.8.pli: diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā”ti.
sn54.4:1.8.eng: enlightenment in this very life, or if there’s something left over, non-return.”

sn54.5:1.1.pli: “Ānāpānassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
sn54.5:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.

sn54.5:1.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?
sn54.5:1.2.eng: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated to be very fruitful and beneficial?

sn54.5:1.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
sn54.5:1.3.eng: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.

sn54.5:1.4.eng: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. …

sn54.5:1.5.eng: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’

sn54.5:1.6.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
sn54.5:1.6.eng: Mindfulness of breathing, when developed and cultivated in this way, is very fruitful and beneficial.

sn54.5:2.1.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitāya kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā.
sn54.5:2.1.eng: When mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated in this way you can expect seven fruits and benefits.

sn54.5:2.3.eng: You attain enlightenment early on in this very life.

sn54.5:2.4.pli: no ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti.

sn54.5:2.5.eng: If not, you attain enlightenment at the time of death.

sn54.5:2.6.pli: no ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti.

sn54.5:2.7.pli: Atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti …
sn54.5:2.7.eng: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters you’re extinguished in between one life and the next …

sn54.5:2.11.eng: you head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm …

sn54.5:2.12.pli: evaṁ bhāvitāya kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya ime satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā”ti.
sn54.5:2.12.eng: When mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated in this way you can expect these seven fruits and benefits.”

sn54.6:1.3.eng: “Mendicants, do you develop mindfulness of breathing?”

sn54.6:1.4.pli: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā ariṭṭho bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn54.6:1.4.eng: When he said this, Venerable Ariṭṭha said to him:

sn54.6:1.6.pli: “Yathā kathaṁ pana tvaṁ, ariṭṭha, bhāvesi ānāpānassatin”ti?
sn54.6:1.6.eng: “But mendicant, how do you develop it?”

sn54.6:1.7.pli: “Atītesu me, bhante, kāmesu kāmacchando pahīno, anāgatesu me kāmesu kāmacchando vigato, ajjhattabahiddhā ca me dhammesu paṭighasaññā suppaṭivinītā.
sn54.6:1.7.eng: “Sir, I’ve given up desire for sensual pleasures of the past. I’m rid of desire for sensual pleasures of the future. And I have eliminated perception of repulsion regarding phenomena internally and externally.

sn54.6:1.8.eng: Just mindful, I will breathe in. Mindful, I will breathe out.

sn54.6:1.9.eng: That’s how I develop mindfulness of breathing.”

sn54.6:2.1.eng: “That is mindfulness of breathing, Ariṭṭha; I don’t deny it.

sn54.6:2.2.pli: Api ca, ariṭṭha, yathā ānāpānassati vitthārena paripuṇṇā hoti
sn54.6:2.2.eng: But as to how mindfulness of breathing is fulfilled in detail,

sn54.6:2.3.pli: taṁ suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
sn54.6:2.3.eng: listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”

sn54.6:2.4.pli: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ariṭṭho bhagavato paccassosi.

sn54.6:3.1.eng: “And how is mindfulness of breathing fulfilled in detail?

sn54.6:3.2.pli: Idha, ariṭṭha, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
sn54.6:3.2.eng: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.

sn54.6:3.3.eng: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out.

sn54.6:3.4.eng: Breathing in heavily they know: ‘I’m breathing in heavily.’ Breathing out heavily they know: ‘I’m breathing out heavily.’ …

sn54.6:3.5.eng: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’

sn54.6:3.6.pli: Evaṁ kho, ariṭṭha, ānāpānassati vitthārena paripuṇṇā hotī”ti.
sn54.6:3.6.eng: This is how mindfulness of breathing is fulfilled in detail.”

sn54.7:1.2.pli: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā mahākappino bhagavato avidūre nisinno hoti pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
sn54.7:1.2.eng: Now at that time Venerable Mahākappina was sitting not far from the Buddha, cross-legged, his body set straight, and mindfulness established in his presence.

sn54.7:1.3.pli: Addasā kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ mahākappinaṁ avidūre nisinnaṁ pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.

sn54.7:2.1.pli: “Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, etassa bhikkhuno kāyassa iñjitattaṁ vā phanditattaṁ vā”ti?
sn54.7:2.1.eng: “Mendicants, do you see any disturbance or trembling in that mendicant’s body?”

sn54.7:2.2.pli: “Yadāpi mayaṁ, bhante, taṁ āyasmantaṁ passāma saṅghamajjhe vā nisinnaṁ ekaṁ vā raho nisinnaṁ, tadāpi mayaṁ tassa āyasmato na passāma kāyassa iñjitattaṁ vā phanditattaṁ vā”ti.
sn54.7:2.2.eng: “Sir, whenever we see that mendicant meditating—whether in the middle of the Saṅgha or alone in private—we never see any disturbance or trembling in his body.”

sn54.7:3.1.pli: “Yassa, bhikkhave, samādhissa bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā neva kāyassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā, na cittassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā, tassa so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu samādhissa nikāmalābhī akicchalābhī akasiralābhī.
sn54.7:3.1.eng: “Mendicants, when an immersion has been developed and cultivated there’s no disturbance or trembling of the body or mind. That mendicant gets such immersion when he wants, without trouble or difficulty.

sn54.7:3.2.pli: Katamassa ca, bhikkhave, samādhissa bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā neva kāyassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā, na cittassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā?

sn54.7:4.1.pli: Ānāpānassatisamādhissa, bhikkhave, bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā neva kāyassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā, na cittassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā.
sn54.7:4.1.eng: When immersion due to mindfulness of breathing has been developed and cultivated there’s no disturbance or trembling of the body or mind.

sn54.7:4.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvite ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhimhi kathaṁ bahulīkate neva kāyassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā, na cittassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā?
sn54.7:4.2.eng: And how is immersion due to mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated in such a way?

sn54.7:5.1.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
sn54.7:5.1.eng: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.

sn54.7:5.2.eng: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. …

sn54.7:5.3.eng: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’

sn54.7:5.4.pli: Evaṁ bhāvite ca kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatisamādhimhi evaṁ bahulīkate neva kāyassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā, na cittassa iñjitattaṁ vā hoti phanditattaṁ vā”ti.
sn54.7:5.4.eng: That’s how immersion due to mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated so that there’s no disturbance or trembling of the body or mind.”
